requirement_summary: Descriptor contracts are immutable, closed-world, deeply snapshotted, and registry hashes cover descriptor-visible identity, schema, capability, artifact, side-effect, idempotency, timeout, and output policy fields.
notes: Validated by descriptor rejection, deep snapshot, hash stability, hash drift, and golden vector tests; no connector, preset, or execution admission is implied.

requirement_summary: Registry registration freezes to an exact-version lookup snapshot with deterministic snapshot_id and snapshot_sha256 and fail-closed missing, invalid, alias, and latest lookup outcomes.
notes: Snapshot rows point at ToolRegistry and FrozenToolRegistrySnapshot behavior, including duplicate rejection, post-freeze rejection, exact lookup, invalid lookup, redacted evidence, and immutable hash records.

requirement_summary: Registry descriptors project through RawToolDescriptor, ToolCatalogEntry, ToolBindingRef, semantic compilation, and lowering without creating a parallel compiler binding model.
notes: The registry projection test checks compiler plans preserve registry-computed descriptor hashes.

requirement_summary: The production built-in descriptor catalog exposes exactly the accepted built-in descriptors, deterministic helpers, descriptor hashes, and snapshot projection without tool execution side effects.
notes: Coverage includes built-in identity, uniqueness, schema validation, deterministic snapshot construction, compiler fixtures, and descriptor-only import behavior.

requirement_summary: Built-in descriptor schema and hash payloads are deterministic, use closed JSON object schemas, and avoid arbitrary shell command, cwd, env map, host-path, daemon-control, or connector fields.
notes: Schema and golden-hash tests pin deterministic descriptor data and prove shell, workspace, and terminal schemas stay constrained.

requirement_summary: Capability declarations, produced artifact IDs, side-effect classes, and idempotency classes for built-ins are exact and aggregate through compiler capability and artifact validation.
notes: Rows map to concrete descriptor constants and compiler validation tests rather than a broad catalog citation.

requirement_summary: Tool execution resolves only exact compiled binding identity: tool_id, tool_version, descriptor_sha256, implementation_id, model tool name, descriptor projection, capabilities, artifacts, side-effect class, and idempotency.
notes: Execution-boundary tests cover exact binding success, uncompiled calls, ambiguous names, projection mismatches, stale hashes, and missing runtime implementations before implementation entry.

requirement_summary: Model arguments are validated against descriptor input schemas before implementation entry, and implementation outputs are validated, redacted, bounded, and hashed after execution.
notes: Coverage includes invalid argument denial, extra-field rejection, output validation failure, secret redaction, host path redaction, truncation, and sanitized output hashing.

requirement_summary: Workspace tools enforce logical relative path containment and reject traversal, absolute paths, Windows drive, UNC, device, ADS, reserved-name, symlink escape, and current-directory violations.
notes: Path-policy tests use local temporary roots and do not depend on live providers, network, credentials, or Millrace daemon state.

requirement_summary: Artifact tools are confined to declared artifact policy under the runtime artifact root and return structured artifact references and hashes instead of arbitrary host paths or unbounded content.
notes: Artifact containment coverage is tied to built-in runtime implementation tests and execution preflight gates.

requirement_summary: Shell/process tools admit only source-owned named profiles and selectors, reject arbitrary command-shaped inputs, and route through bounded process profile policy.
notes: Schema tests reject arbitrary shell fields and runtime tests cover approved profile execution policy without network or package-install behavior.

requirement_summary: Terminal tools emit validated Millforge TerminalIntent outputs only and do not mutate Millrace queue, status, daemon, tmux, git, or external release state.
notes: Terminal boundary tests assert intent shape and authority separation; this row deliberately does not claim Millrace runner integration.

requirement_summary: Trace completeness requires every attempted or denied tool call to emit a redacted ToolTraceRecord with sequence, request/run/stage identity, binding resolution status, input hash, decisions, status, side-effect data, timing, and bounded summary.
notes: Trace tests validate success and denial records, binding-resolution statuses, redaction, and sanitized summaries.

requirement_summary: Ambiguous side-effect failures and non-idempotent terminal behaviors are classified conservatively and are not marked retryable after unknown completion.
notes: Implementation-error handling records completion_unknown, ambiguous_side_effect detail, and retry_allowed=false.

requirement_summary: Spec 04 closure includes a machine-checkable conformance matrix that maps 04A-04D requirements to concrete files, tests, offline evidence commands, status, and conservative notes.
notes: This row is self-checking and prevents citation-only pass rows by requiring concrete implementation files, test modules, and offline commands.

requirement_summary: Package and public API boundaries expose accepted 04A-04C contracts and create_builtin_tool_executor while rejecting production presets, connector broker/admission, Millrace runner, daemon, queue/status control, local/private defaults, and private runtime state in archives.
notes: The matrix claims public API and package audit coverage only; it does not mark any future preset, connector admission, Millrace runner, eval, or live provider behavior as implemented.

requirement_summary: Connector-shaped descriptors are readiness-tested through generic registry and compiler catalog behavior without admitting connectors, MCP discovery, brokers, identity manifests, approval flow, custom compilation, or live connector execution.
evidence_commands: python -m pytest tests/test_tool_registry_closure.py tests/test_tool_registry.py tests/test_tool_execution_boundary.py tests/compiler
notes: Generic connector-shaped descriptor projection can be tested as readiness evidence, but Spec 05 owns connector admission, discovery, brokers, identity manifests, MCP, approvals, custom compilation, and live execution.

requirement_summary: Closure evidence remains offline, deterministic, bounded, and independent of OpenAI, DeepSeek, MCP, local credentials, live provider state, live connector state, network, and Millrace daemon state.
evidence_commands: python -m pytest tests/test_tool_registry_closure.py tests/test_tool_execution_boundary.py tests/test_builtin_tool_catalog.py tests/test_tool_registry.py tests/compiler
notes: The validator rejects command strings that invoke network, providers, credentials, MCP, connectors, Millrace daemon control, or external package installation.

requirement_summary: Millrace runner binding, queue/status authority, daemon controls, and workflow runtime mutation remain outside Spec 04 closure.
notes: Deferred to Spec 01; this row exists so implemented rows cannot overclaim Millrace runner integration.

requirement_summary: Connector discovery, connector identity manifests, connector brokers, MCP, approvals, admitted connector execution, and custom tool compilation remain outside Spec 04 closure.
notes: Deferred to Spec 05; Spec 04 may prove generic descriptor readiness but does not admit connectors.

requirement_summary: Production Planner, Builder, Checker, Arbiter, or eval harness presets remain outside Spec 04 closure.
notes: Deferred to Spec 07; existing built-in compiler fixtures are readiness evidence, not production preset implementation.

requirement_summary: Small-model workflow behavior and comparative evaluation campaigns remain outside Spec 04 closure.
notes: Deferred to Specs 06 and 08; closure tests stay offline and do not claim live model or eval-suite execution.
